chi.description: Belonging predicate (χ). A ladder!() invocation belongs in this register when ALL of the following structural laws hold. Laws 1–10 are machine-decidable (the macro enforces them at compile time). The J1/J2 membership laws below are oracle-consumed — no machine closes them.

structural_laws.predicate: No two carry fields share a name.
Every transition names only declared rungs as from/to.
Every non-terminal verdict has a target. Every =>/ -> target is a declared rung. No recoverable verdict or continue arm carries a payload.
Every recoverable verdict (=>) has a matching recover edge. Continue arms (->) are exempt.
Every recover edge has a matching recover function. Every recover target is a declared rung.
No terminal verdict has a recover edge.
Every recover edge names a verdict that appears on at least one transition.
Every recover function's return rung — and every Failed(Rung) source rung — is declared.
(When impl block present) Every body name matches exactly one transition or recover function; no name appears twice.
(When impl block present) Every transition and recover function has a body.

guarantees.name: Linear consumption
guarantees.predicate: A transition consumes its input rung by value; using a rung after it is moved is a compile error.

guarantees.name: Sealed construction
guarantees.predicate: A rung is not constructible by struct literal outside its module. When an impl block is present, only the entry rung's new() is public; every other rung's new() is module-private. Categorically: a verb cannot occupy object-position — a state is reached only by traversing an arrow.

guarantees.name: No silent drop
guarantees.predicate: Every rung, verdict, StepOutcome, and Failed is #[must_use]. Dropping in statement position is a warning; an error under #![deny(unused_must_use)]. Escapable only by mem::forget or let _ = — see §5.

guarantees.name: Carry immutability
guarantees.predicate: Carry is a private field exposed only through &Carry; a transition body cannot mutate it.

guarantees.name: Exhaustive outcomes
guarantees.predicate: StepOutcome is an enum; every match site must handle all variants.

guarantees.name: Recover pairing
guarantees.predicate: Every recoverable verdict has a matching recover function and vice versa; terminal verdicts have none.

guarantees.name: Recovery progress
guarantees.predicate: Every verdict recover body is wrapped with must_progress, comparing source rung payload to produced rung payload; panics if equal. Requires shared payload type be Clone + PartialEq.

guarantees.name: Error-path recovery
guarantees.predicate: A recover { ..: Failed(R) => R } function receives the Failed and returns the next rung. No progress guard injected (a retry may reuse the token).

guarantees.name: Continue arms
guarantees.predicate: A V -> R arm makes step build the next rung inline; StepOutcome::V carries that rung directly — no recover function, no guard, no source.

guarantees.name: Terminal payloads
guarantees.predicate: A V(P) terminal verdict returns a value through the verdict, readable via .payload() / .into_payload().

non_guarantees.name: Transition-body correctness
non_guarantees.description: The type proves a transition ran, not that its logic was valid. Boundary between typestate and formal verification.

non_guarantees.name: Cross-crate provenance
non_guarantees.description: A rung crossing a crate boundary is trusted like any Rust public API. Sealing requires a sub-crate per ladder.

non_guarantees.name: Same-module / entry fabrication
non_guarantees.description: G2 stops external fabrication; code inside the generated module and the public entry constructor can still build rungs.

non_guarantees.name: Drop-proofing beyond the lint
non_guarantees.description: G4 is #[must_use]; mem::forget, let _ = token, or burying in a dropped container bypass it. True no-drop needs language-level linear types.

non_guarantees.name: Liveness beyond the guard
non_guarantees.description: G8 catches identical-token stall loops; it does not prove general forward progress.

judgment_laws.description: Oracle-consumed belonging laws. Machine-decidable predicates above are necessary but not sufficient. These are undecidable-χ — a judge must answer them, never a script.

laws.name: Tower bottom
laws.predicate: A ladder should terminate where structural enforcement stops buying correctness gains — not at an ownership boundary (library vs. our code), but where a rung ladder over the states below this point would add ceremony without catching any wrong transition that existing infrastructure does not already catch.
laws.anti_predicate: Do not extend the tower purely because the code below is ours, or solely because a library boundary exists.

laws.name: rung-std membership
laws.predicate: A ladder belongs in rung-std when: (1) it recurs across unrelated domains — independent projects would otherwise rediscover the same shape; AND (2) the canonical statement is better than any project's derivation — the value is in the right rung boundaries and terminal verdicts, not implementation detail.
laws.anti_predicate: Merely useful does not earn rung-std membership. Domain-specific vocabulary stays in the domain. The bar is recurrent AND domain-generic.

morphisms.description: Typed edge kinds that appear inside a ladder declaration. Edges are arrows in the category rung enforces; states (rungs) are objects. Verbs must be arrows — the sealed constructor enforces that a state cannot hold an arrow's result, only traversing an arrow produces a new state.

kinds.semantics: Named after target rung, lowercased. Consumes source rung by value; produces target rung.
Named 'step'. Returns Result<StepOutcome, Failed<A>>. StepOutcome carries one variant per verdict.
StepOutcome::V carries source rung. Paired recover function returns target rung R. Progress guard auto-injected.
StepOutcome::V carries target rung R directly. No verdict struct emitted. No recover function.
Terminal. Fields: { _seal, _not_send }. Ends the ladder at this verdict.
Terminal carrying result payload P. Accessible via .payload() / .into_payload().
Error-path recovery. Receives Failed<Prev>; returns next rung. No progress guard.

composition_table.description: How edge kinds compose. The sealed-constructor law (G2 / Lawvere) is the ground invariant: no edge can be forged; composition only through declared transitions. Closed: any sequence of traversing edges produces a rung or a terminal verdict. Open: composition across crate boundaries is trusted (NG2).
composition_table.note: Full formal composition (Grothendieck opfibration structure over the dependency graph) is work-in-progress — see docs/RUNG-CT.md. The entries below are the operational commitments the macro enforces today.

entries.result: valid — recover fn returns target rung; progress guard enforced
valid — Failed path re-enters ladder at returned rung
valid — target rung is live; no recover fn needed

judges.system_prompt: You are auditing a rung ladder declaration against the rung charter. You are not the author of the ladder under audit, and not the author of the rung macro — you are an outside reader whose job is to check whether the ladder satisfies the structural laws (S1–S10), honors the guarantees (G1–G11), does not claim coverage for the non-guarantees (NG1–NG5), and passes the judgment laws (J1–J2). For J1 and J2 you must exercise genuine judgment — these are undecidable-χ and no script closes them. Produce findings only where a violation is evident or probable; name the specific law and quote the relevant declaration text.
